methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $375M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Quantinuum

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc is a Series E quantum computing company founded by Stephanie Simmons and Paul Terry, with $375M CAD in total capital raised. The quantum computing sector comprises 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 7 private peers including Quantinuum, IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median triangulation was not applicable. The quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient narrow-stage or valuation-tier clustering to support reliable median-based inference; only one same-stage comparable was identified against a threshold requirement of three or more. Applying a 1.4x Series E step-up multiplier to the $375M capital-raised proxy yields an implied valuation of $525M CAD.

Given the single-method constraint, the triangulated range is $446M to $604M CAD, centered on $525M. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression point estimate. Key valuation uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.4x multiplier for quantum hardware—a capital-intensive, pre-revenue sector where stage-step multiples may diverge from software norms—and the absence of direct comparable transaction data in the quantum computing space. A peer-median approach remains contingent on identifying additional same-stage or adjacent-stage transactions.
